subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Board Tiles
- **Location tiles** — 16 double-sided tiles forming a 4x4 bazaar grid.
- **Locations include** — Wainwright, Fabric Warehouse, Spice Warehouse, Fruit Warehouse, Post Office, Caravansary, Sultan's Palace, Small Market, Large Market, Police Station, Fountain, Black Market, Tea House, Small Mosque, Great Mosque, Gemstone Dealer.

#### Player Components (per player)
- **Merchant disc** — 1 large disc (your merchant).
- **Assistant discs** — 4 small discs (your assistants).
- **Wheelbarrow tile** — 1 tile showing goods capacity (starts with space for 2 of each good, expandable).
- **Wheelbarrow extensions** — 3 extension tiles (purchased at Wainwright).

#### Other Components
- **Rubies** — Gemstone tokens (the victory objective).
- **Goods indicators** — Tokens for fabric, spices, fruit, and blue jewelry on wheelbarrows.
- **Coins (Lira)** — Various denominations.
- **Bonus cards** — Dealt at Caravansary.
- **Demand tiles** — For Small and Large Market.
- **Mosque tiles** — Ability tiles at mosques.
- **Family member** — 1 neutral pawn per player.
- **Governor & Smuggler** — 2 neutral pawns placed on the board.

subtopics.title: Board Layout
subtopics.content:
#### Grid Setup
1. **Arrange tiles** — Place the 16 location tiles in a 4x4 grid. For first game, use the recommended layout from the rulebook. For experienced players, randomize.
2. **Place Governor** — Put the Governor pawn on the Police Station tile.
3. **Place Smuggler** — Put the Smuggler pawn on the Police Station tile.
4. **Market demand** — Randomly place demand tiles on the Small Market and Large Market.
5. **Mosque tiles** — Place 2 Mosque ability tiles on each Mosque (Small Mosque and Great Mosque), accessible side up.
6. **Rubies** — Place rubies on the Sultan's Palace, Gemstone Dealer, Small Mosque, and Great Mosque as indicated.
7. **Coin supply** — Place Lira near the board.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
#### Per-Player Setup
1. **Merchant and assistants** — Stack your merchant disc on top of your 4 assistant discs to form a stack.
2. **Starting position** — Place your entire stack on the Fountain tile.
3. **Wheelbarrow** — Take your wheelbarrow tile. Starting capacity: 2 of each good type (fabric, spices, fruit, jewelry).
4. **Starting goods** — Begin with 0 goods on your wheelbarrow.
5. **Starting money** — Receive starting Lira based on player order: 1st player gets 2 Lira, 2nd gets 3 Lira, 3rd gets 4 Lira, etc.
6. **Family member** — Place your family member pawn on the Police Station.
7. **Rubies** — Start with 0 rubies.

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content:
#### First Player
- **Random selection** — Choose a starting player randomly.
- **Turn order** — Play proceeds clockwise.

#### Ruby Target
- **2-3 players** — First to collect 5 rubies wins.
- **4 players** — First to collect 5 rubies wins.
- **5 players** — First to collect 5 rubies wins.

#### Starting State
- All players' stacks begin at the Fountain.
- All family members begin at the Police Station.
- Governor and Smuggler begin at the Police Station.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
#### On Your Turn
1. **Move** — Move your merchant (and stack) 1 or 2 spaces orthogonally (not diagonally). You must move; you cannot stay in place.
2. **Drop off or pick up an assistant** — At your destination, you must either leave 1 assistant from your stack OR pick up 1 of your assistants already at that location. You cannot take a location's action without this exchange.
3. **Perform location action** — Carry out the action of the tile you landed on.
4. **Encounter other merchants** — If other players' merchants (not assistants) are on the same tile, you may pay them 2 Lira each, or they catch a ride (no payment needed in some variants). If you land on the Governor or Smuggler, you may interact with them.

#### Assistant Management
- **Running out** — If you have no assistants left in your stack and no assistants at your destination, you cannot take actions at most locations.
- **Fountain** — Moving to the Fountain picks up ALL your assistants from everywhere on the board, returning them to your stack.

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
#### Warehouse Locations
- **Fabric Warehouse** — Fill your wheelbarrow with fabric to maximum capacity.
- **Spice Warehouse** — Fill your wheelbarrow with spices to maximum capacity.
- **Fruit Warehouse** — Fill your wheelbarrow with fruit to maximum capacity.

#### Market Locations
- **Small Market / Large Market** — Sell specific combinations of goods for Lira as shown on the demand tile. After selling, roll the demand die to set new prices.

#### Key Locations
- **Wainwright** — Pay 7 Lira to add a wheelbarrow extension (+1 capacity for each good type). After 3 extensions, gain 1 ruby.
- **Sultan's Palace** — Deliver a specific combination of goods to receive 1 ruby. Required goods increase with each ruby taken.
- **Gemstone Dealer** — Pay Lira to buy 1 ruby. Price increases with each purchase (12, 13, 14... Lira).
- **Small Mosque / Great Mosque** — Pay specific goods to gain Mosque tiles (permanent abilities). Collecting 2 tiles from one Mosque earns 1 ruby.
- **Post Office** — Gain a fixed set of goods and/or Lira.
- **Caravansary** — Draw 2 bonus cards, keep 1.
- **Tea House** — Name a number (3-12), another player rolls 2 dice; if the roll meets or exceeds your number, gain that many Lira.
- **Black Market** — Gain 1 good of your choice plus roll dice for a bonus good. Can sell blue jewelry here.
- **Police Station** — Send your family member to any location to use that location's action.
- **Fountain** — Recall all your assistants back to your stack.

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
#### Governor & Smuggler
- **Governor** — If you land on the Governor's tile, you may pay 2 Lira to gain 1 bonus card or use the Governor to take any 1 good.
- **Smuggler** — If you land on the Smuggler's tile, you may pay 2 Lira to gain 1 good of your choice.
- **Movement** — After interacting, move the Governor/Smuggler to any other tile.

#### Family Member
- From the Police Station, send your family member to any tile to perform that tile's action remotely.
- Your family member stays at that tile. If another player's merchant lands on your family member, they must pay you 2 Lira and your family member returns to the Police Station.

#### Mosque Abilities
- **Small Mosque tiles** — Reroll dice at markets, extra Lira at Tea House, etc.
- **Great Mosque tiles** — Return an assistant to your stack, gain goods when using Fountain, etc.
- Collecting both tiles from either Mosque earns a ruby.

#### Bonus Cards
- Gained at Caravansary. Provide one-time effects: free goods, extra movement, Lira, or can be used as wild goods at Sultan's Palace.

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### Winning
- **Race to rubies** — The game ends immediately when a player collects their 5th ruby.
- That player wins. There is no final round.

#### Ruby Sources (6 possible)
1. **Sultan's Palace** — Deliver goods.
2. **Gemstone Dealer** — Pay increasing Lira.
3. **Wainwright** — After buying all 3 extensions.
4. **Small Mosque** — Collect both tiles.
5. **Great Mosque** — Collect both tiles.
6. **Collecting 5 bonus cards** — Grants 1 ruby.

#### Tiebreaker
- If two players reach 5 rubies in the same round, the player who reached it first (earlier in turn order that round) wins. Then: most Lira, most goods.

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
#### Early Game
- **Wainwright first** — Expanding your wheelbarrow early lets you carry more goods for the rest of the game, and completing all 3 extensions earns a ruby.
- **Warehouse route** — Plan a path that efficiently loops through warehouses, markets, and ruby locations.
- **Mosque tiles early** — Their permanent abilities compound over many turns.
- **Manage assistants** — Minimize wasted trips to the Fountain. Plan routes that pick up assistants as you go.

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
#### Frequent Errors
- **Running out of assistants** — If you leave all assistants behind with no pickup route, you waste turns going to the Fountain.
- **Ignoring the Gemstone Dealer** — Buying rubies for Lira is straightforward and should not be overlooked.
- **Tunnel vision on one ruby source** — You need 5 rubies from different sources. Diversify your approach.
- **Forgetting family member** — The family member is a free remote action that many players underuse.
- **Poor movement planning** — Moving 2 spaces is often better than 1. Plan 2-3 turns ahead to minimize wasted movement.

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content:
#### Critical Choices
- **Wheelbarrow vs. speed** — Fully upgrading the wheelbarrow (3 visits + Lira) gives 1 ruby but costs tempo. Sometimes 2 extensions is enough.
- **Which Mosque tiles** — Choose tiles that synergize with your planned route and strategy.
- **When to Fountain** — Return for assistants only when your route truly requires it, not prematurely.
- **Sultan's Palace timing** — Goods requirements increase per ruby. Early Sultan visits are cheaper.
- **Blocking opponents** — Landing on opponents' merchants costs them 2 Lira. Positioning to block key locations is strong in tight games.
